title: Sprite Semantic Dictionary
description: Machine-readable semantic region map for canonical Asciicker sprites. Each file maps one sprite family's reference XP to named body regions, palette roles, and frame-local cell coordinates so that agents can safely execute semantic edits like 'make the shirt green' without touching unrelated regions.

family.description: Sprite family name matching the bundle template system

reference_xp.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical reference XP file

semantic_layer.description: 0-based layer index containing the visual appearance data (typically L2)

frame_w.description: Width of a single frame in cells

frame_h.description: Height of a single frame in cells

anim_counts.description: Frame count per animation sequence (e.g. [1, 8] = 1 idle + 8 walk)

rows.description: Total angle rows in the sheet

palette_roles.description: Named color roles with their representative hex colors. Keys are role names, values are role descriptors.

colors.description: Representative hex colors for this role (may appear as fg or bg)

confidence.description: Confidence level based on cross-frame verification

usage.description: Whether this color typically appears as foreground, background, or both

description.description: Human-readable description of this palette role

frames.description: Per-frame semantic region maps. Keys are frame indices as strings.

name.description: Semantic region name (e.g. face, shirt, pants, boots, weapon)

bbox.description: Bounding box in frame-local coords [x0, y0, x1, y1] inclusive

palette_roles.description: Palette role names active in this region

role.description: Semantic role of this specific cell

semantic_cells.description: Individually identified semantic cells within the region

ambiguities.description: Known ambiguities that require human verification

transparent_bg.description: Background color used for transparent/empty cells (REXPaint convention)
